mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in more than 55% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a win rate above 55% in walk-forward testing, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and slowing economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 50% of declining INDPRO periods during tightening cycles, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 295, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing aggressive central bank intervention that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where CPIAUCSL surpasses the 295 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative win rate below 50% over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that increases corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of expanding GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side contraction that reduces industrial output and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances where industrial production falls below its 12-month average.
